markdown.markdown:
::: {.hero-section style="background-image: url('images/certificate-thumbnail.jpg')"} ::: {.hero-badge} UC Riverside Library · Self-Paced Certificate ::: Practical skills for every stage of your research journey — citation management, scholarly communication, data management, literature review, publishing, and more. **Complete all modules at your own pace to earn your certificate.** ::: ## What You'll Learn The **Graduate Research Core Competencies Certificate** is designed to support graduate students at all stages of their research journey by building practical, transferable skills essential to scholarly work. Participants develop strategies for: - **Effective citation management** using tools like Zotero and Paperpile - **Advanced literature review techniques** including systematic and scoping reviews - **Responsible research data management** and open data publishing - **Scholarly communication** — ORCID, researcher identity, and open access - **Publishing & sharing** theses, dissertations, and research articles - **Graduation requirements** — formatting, ProQuest, and Graduate Division workflows > The modules can be completed **in any order**, at your own pace. A certificate will be awarded upon completion of all modules. --- ```{=html}
Submit Assignments on Canvas to Earn Points

The knowledge checks on this website mirror the quizzes on Canvas and are for self-study practice. To receive credit toward your certificate, complete and submit all assignments through the Canvas course platform.

``` ## Course Modules ```{=html}
Module 1

Citation Management

Master Zotero and Paperpile to organize your references and create biosketches for grant applications.

ZoteroPaperpileBiosketch
Start Module →
Module 2

Scholarly Communication

Build your academic identity with ORCID, explore open access publishing, and understand eScholarship.

ORCIDOpen AccesseScholarship
Start Module →
Module 3

Data Management

Create data management plans, organize your research data, and publish with Dryad.

DMP ToolDryadFAIR Data
Start Module →
Module 4

Literature Review Strategies

Design effective search strategies, understand types of reviews, and use UC Library resources.

Database SearchSystematic ReviewAnnotated Bibliography
Start Module →
Module 5

Publish & Share Your Research

Navigate copyright, fair use, open access options, and the path from dissertation to publication.

CopyrightFair UseOpen Access
Start Module →
Module 6

Graduate Academic Affairs

Understand UCR formatting requirements, the ProQuest submission process, and graduation timelines.

ProQuestFormattingGraduation
Start Module →
Module 7 · Capstone

Research Plan

Integrate all competencies into a comprehensive research plan — your capstone for the certificate.

CapstoneResearch Plan🎓 Certificate
Start Module →
``` --- ## How to Earn Your Certificate :::: {.columns} ::: {.column width="30%"} ### 📖 Step 1 **Complete each module** — read the content, explore resources, and watch the videos. ::: ::: {.column width="5%"} ::: ::: {.column width="30%"} ### ✏️ Step 2 **Pass the knowledge check** at the end of each module (where provided). ::: ::: {.column width="5%"} ::: ::: {.column width="30%"} ### 🎓 Step 3 **Submit the Capstone Research Plan** to your instructor to receive your official certificate via Accredible. ::: :::: --- ## Get Help Need additional support? Contact UCR Library experts: | Topic | Contact | |---|---| | Citation Management | [libstemteam@ucr.edu](mailto:libstemteam@ucr.edu) | | Scholarly Communication & Open Access | Jing Han · [jingh@ucr.edu](mailto:jingh@ucr.edu) | | Data Management & Data Services | Dr. Barbara Martinez Neda · [barbara.martinezneda@ucr.edu](mailto:barbara.martinezneda@ucr.edu) | | Literature Review | [UCR Library Teaching & Learning Services](https://library.ucr.edu/about/directory/staff) | | Thesis/Dissertation Formatting | [UCR Graduate Division](https://graduate.ucr.edu/filing-resources) | **Upcoming Workshops:** [library.ucr.edu](https://library.ucr.edu)
